title: QED-C Announces Research Advances In Quantum Control Electronics
why:
- QED-C announced advancements in quantum control electronics through a research program supported by the National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST).
- $1.4 million in matching funds was awarded in 2022 to member companies—Amphenol RF, Maybell Quantum Industries, Rigetti Computing, and XMA—to support the quantum supply chain.
- Results demonstrate improvements in size and performance across multiple qubit modalities, such as reduced cabling footprints and on-chip temperature measurement.
- These advances align with four key QED-C roadmap goals, including reducing thermal load and the physical footprint of microwave-control cabling in cryogenic environments, and situating digital electronics closer to the quantum processor.
- Innovations include Amphenol RF reducing the size, weight, and signal loss of control electronics, Maybell Quantum Industries achieving tighter integration of devices, Rigetti Computing enabling on-chip temperature measurement, and XMA increasing channel capacity while decreasing cabling size and cost.
